task.title: Public profile generation works with LinkAPI and writes in-character profile fields
task.source: User report: LinkAPI returned no assistant text/tool calls and generated handles, bios, and tags were not in character.

coreClaim: Character maker public profile generation requests JSON output and frames public handle, bio, and tags as the character's own self-presentation.
rootCause: The character maker asked for JSON only in prose and described public profile fields as generic catalog preview text, leaving OpenAI-compatible providers such as LinkAPI more room to return non-final or empty assistant text and encouraging out-of-character summary fields.

inScope: Character maker prompt contract for publicProfile fields
Focused regression coverage for JSON mode and in-character public profile wording
outOfScope: Roleplay or game mode generation routing

claimBoundary.coreClaim: Character maker public profile requests now include JSON object mode and in-character public-profile instructions.
legacyPathsOrFormats: OpenAI-compatible providers without JSON-mode support still receive the explicit JSON-only prompt text.
Google/Gemini LinkAPI-style connections use provider-specific generationConfig handling and may ignore responseFormat while preserving prompt instructions.
Existing Rust provider parser behavior remains unchanged for providers that return assistant text normally.
outOfScopeClaims: This PR does not prove a live LinkAPI response because no external provider call was run in this shipping flow.
This PR does not change Rust provider parsing or provider endpoint selection.
This PR does not change persona or lorebook prompt content beyond the shared JSON-mode request parameter used by JSON makers.

contractLaneGate.brokenContract: The character maker requested a JSON character card through prompt prose only and did not require public profile fields to be character-authored.
contractLaneGate.consumer: src/shared/api/llm-api.ts and OpenAI-compatible provider transports including LinkAPI-style chat completions
contractLaneGate.impliedContract: JSON maker requests should communicate both machine-readable JSON intent to capable providers and the product semantic contract for generated public profile fields.
contractLaneGate.actualEnforcement: runMakerRequest now sends responseFormat=json_object, and CHARACTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT describes publicProfile handle, bio, and tags as the character's own public self-presentation.
contractLaneGate.primaryOwnerDetail: Engine generation owns maker prompt semantics and LLM request parameters for character creation.
contractLaneGate.wrongLaneFixToAvoid: Do not hide this with a UI-only retry or editor fallback; the generation request contract must be precise before it reaches provider transport.
regressionProof: src/engine/generation/makers.spec.ts asserts character maker requests include responseFormat=json_object.
src/engine/generation/makers.spec.ts asserts the prompt frames publicProfile fields as character-chosen self-presentation.
pnpm check passed after the final pushed diff.

positiveRows.claim: Character maker requests JSON object output for provider compatibility.
positiveRows.evidence: pnpm vitest run src/engine/generation/makers.spec.ts --pool=threads --maxWorkers=1 --no-file-parallelism passed with 7 tests.

positiveRows.claim: Generated public profile fields are prompted as in-character choices.
positiveRows.evidence: The focused maker spec asserts the prompt contains 'chosen by the character as their own public self-presentation' and 'Write the handle, bio, and public tags in the character's voice'.

positiveRows.claim: The change preserves TypeScript and architecture boundaries.
positiveRows.evidence: pnpm typecheck and pnpm check:architecture passed after the branch was rebased onto origin/main.

positiveRows.claim: The PR baseline gate is green locally.
positiveRows.evidence: pnpm check passed, including line endings, scratch, architecture, storage contracts, frontend, Rust, docs, discovery, agent workflow, launcher safety, and warning-only unused-code reporting.

contradictionRows.claim: The fix should not rely on broad provider parser changes or fake success handling.
contradictionRows.evidence: The diff is limited to src/engine/generation/makers.ts, src/engine/generation/makers.spec.ts, and this proof ledger; no Rust provider parser or UI fallback changed.

contradictionRows.claim: The public profile prompt should still avoid private creator notes and hidden twists.
contradictionRows.evidence: The updated bio field keeps the existing instruction: 'Do not include creator notes, hidden twists, or private setup instructions.'

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Providers that already returned valid JSON text keep the same visible prompt contract with stricter publicProfile wording.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: The existing maker tests for non-streaming and streaming visible JSON still pass in src/engine/generation/makers.spec.ts.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Providers that ignore responseFormat are still guided by prompt text rather than a provider-specific fallback.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: CHARACTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT still says Return ONLY valid JSON and now includes the explicit publicProfile object schema.

untestedRows: A live LinkAPI completion was not run from this environment; provider compatibility is proven by request shape and local generation-contract tests.

ownedFacts.description: OpenAI-compatible provider bodies consume responseFormat by mapping it to response_format.type.
Character maker generation is owned by the TypeScript engine, not React UI or Rust provider parsing.
ownedFacts.evidence: src/features/catalog/characters/components/CharacterMakerModal.tsx calls generateCharacterMaker, and the changed prompt/request construction lives in src/engine/generation/makers.ts.

manualBlockers.reason: The core claim is the generated request contract and prompt framing. Live provider behavior remains a useful manual smoke test but is not required to prove the local code path.

verification.baselineEvidence: pnpm check exited 0 after line-ending normalization and re-run from the isolated PR worktree.

notes: The PR remains draft until template text, proof health, CI, and Bunny gates are clean.
